sourceMovie.title: Epic
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Epic for the same reason it broke through in 2013: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a adventure rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Chris Wedge keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The animation and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Anastasia
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Epic clicked for you, Anastasia (1997) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and adventure, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Epic?
faq.answer: Try Anastasia, Wolfwalkers, The Secret of NIMH—each shares DNA with Epic while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Epic?
faq.answer: Epic (2013) is best described as Animation, Adventure, Family, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Epic worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.5/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this animation lane.
